issue.body:
TEST_COVERAGE_WORKPLAN flags deeper line-pairing and character-group fallback branches in `ocr/ground_truth_matching.py` as the remaining gap (currently 84.6%).

Mechanical: write targeted tests for the fallback branches with small hand-built Page / ground-truth fixtures.

Source: TEST_COVERAGE_WORKPLAN.md (Remaining - Ground-truth matching).

## Acceptance
- [ ] `ocr/ground_truth_matching.py` coverage moves above 90%
- [ ] At least one test per identifiable fallback branch
- [ ] `make test` passes

issue.title: ship-issue: rolling work

issue.body:
## Finding

During R4 B6 multi-cycle stress, 3 bot-generated test files passed local `make ci` but failed GitHub CI:

1. **ruff format**: Test files had double blank lines between class definitions. Local pre-commit hook runs `ruff format` on staged files only (incremental), so the full-file check in GitHub CI found additional reformatting needed.

2. **B007** (ruff check): `test_page_training_set_generators.py` used `for i, text in enumerate(words_list)` where `i` was not used. Local pre-commit passed (unknown why — possibly pre-commit ruff check ran on the old cached state), but GitHub CI's full ruff check caught it.

## Fix applied

- `fix(tests): ruff format 3 coverage test files` (commit 76388aa on wip/ship-issue)
- `fix(tests): rename unused loop vars i→_i (B007)` (commit b61a23f on wip/ship-issue)

Both fixes were applied manually after CI failure surfaced them. The orchestrator has no retry loop for CI.

## Remediation options

1. The ship-issue skill prompt should instruct the bot to run `uv run ruff format .` and `uv run ruff check .` (full repo, not just changed files) before committing — not just rely on pre-commit.
2. Alternatively, configure the local pre-commit hook to always run `--all-files` (slower but matches CI).
3. Add a lint-all step to `success.sh` before `make ci`.

## Affected issues

- Issue #8 (page rendering helpers coverage)
- Issue #9 (page training-set generators coverage)  
- Issue #10 (ground_truth_matching fallback branches coverage)

## Severity

**Low** — orchestrator and skill infrastructure works; only the bot's code quality in generated tests is lower than CI requires. Easy to fix in the skill prompt or success.sh.
issue.title: [pilot-feedback] Bot-generated test files fail GitHub CI ruff format/check (pre-commit scope gap)

issue.body:
## Summary

The ship-issue bot, while shipping issues #8/#9/#10/#12 to wip/ship-issue, appended an `### Issue #22 — 2026-05-10 17:27 UTC / Closes #22` section to PR #17's body. Issue #22 ("Child A: test triage-fork.py pointer comment on parent") was already closed and is unrelated to coverage work. Had this PR been merged as-is, GitHub would have re-processed the `Closes #22` (no-op on already-closed issue, but signals the bot wrote bad metadata).

## Repro

`gh pr view 17 -R ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools --json body --jq .body` (before R4 cleanup at 2026-05-11) showed the orphan section.

## Root cause hypothesis

The bot's success.sh appends a PR-body section based on whatever issue number was passed via `ISSUE_JSON` or env var. If that number was leftover from a prior aborted cycle, or read from a stale .ship-issue-tmp/ file, the bot appends without validating.

## Suggested fix

In `scripts/ship-issue-success.sh` (or the skill's PR-body-update step):
1. Validate the issue number belongs to the current repo: `gh issue view $N -R $REPO --json state,number` — must succeed and state must be OPEN.
2. If state is CLOSED or the gh call fails, refuse to append the section + log to stderr; the orchestrator should treat this as a bounce.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Bot refuses to append a PR-body section for issues that are already CLOSED at success time.
- [ ] Bot refuses to append a PR-body section for issues from a different repo.
- [ ] Regression test covers both failure modes.

## Surfaced by

PR #17 static review (R4 closeout, finding J-4).
issue.title: ship-issue: bot appended unrelated/closed issue #22 to PR #17 body

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #10

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#303

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/ocr/page.py:3304`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/page.py:3417`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/page.py:3435`

Training-set image names are built directly from caller-provided `prefix`. Prefixes containing path separators or absolute-path syntax can affect where paths resolve. The recognition path also deletes matching files based on that prefix.

Impact:

- A hostile or accidental prefix can overwrite or delete files outside the intended image directory.

Remediation:

- Restrict `prefix` to a basename-safe character set.
- Reject path separators and absolute prefixes.
- Assert resolved output paths remain under the intended `images/` directory.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: training-set `prefix` can escape output directories
